ops.title: Re-run the concurrency 429 sweep in a clean window (WF-A item 1)
ops.description: Deferred from WF-A (ledger 556d952f2): the 07-19 attempt was confounded — editable install being rewritten mid-measurement (PROMPT_VERSION flipped under it) and sibling lanes sharing the account's rate budget. The rig is built and validated at ~/.cc-steer/experiments/WFA/scripts/ (PATH-first capture shim with the mktemp-collision fix, run_stage.py fresh-clone timing, make_slices.py, table.py); ~$9 of lane budget was preserved. Requirements for a valid run: (a) frozen code (no editable-install churn), (b) this sweep as the SOLE LLM consumer on the account, (c) slices regenerated post-v7 (the v6 slices.json is stale after the re-judge). Question: does concurrency 8→16→24 on triage/refine buy throughput or just 429s? Preliminary inference (unmeasured): TPM-bound past ~8 at ~35K tok/row.

ops.text: sweep run under frozen code as sole consumer, slices regenerated post-v7
throughput + 429/fail% vs concurrency table appended to ledger 556d952f2
DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY recommendation made from measured data
